method_description: National average electricity supply dominated by the incumbent thermal fleet with declining coal share and some gas backup.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: Intended as the declining incumbent benchmark. In Phase 1 this is a national average, not a method-specific dispatch profile.

method_description: National reduced-form frontier broadly consistent with AEMO/CSIRO planning: VRE, storage, transmission and gas backup under implemented-policy decarbonisation.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: 2030 cost anchored to CSIRO GenCost whole-system estimate (~A$91/MWh incl. transmission) and 2050 deep-clean emissions intensity consistent with CSIRO benchmark range.

method_description: More conservative high-firming near-zero power system method with extra storage/firming and very low direct emissions intensity.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: Represents an efficient but more conservatively firmed near-zero electricity method. Useful as a sensitivity method when the model values residual emissions reduction more highly than system cost.
